leadership, for electing me as board president, and for their collaborative spirit. Together we
enacted meaningful policies designed to safeguard our children and strengthen our district.

[03:00–03:30]

policies to ensure curriculum and library materials are appropriate and educationally suitable, and
measures to limit cell phone use in our schools to foster a better learning environment. During my
presidency, I asked more of trustees, greater time commitments, deeper engagement through board
committees, and participation in two board meetings per month. I am deeply appreciative of the hard
work.

[03:30–04:00]

district leadership. A board without such committees risks becoming a mere rubber stamp, failing in
its oversight role. Regardless of the talent within our district administration, it is the board's
responsibility to

[04:30–05:00]
To engage, scrutinize, and contribute solutions with a strong work ethic. As I conclude my service,
I would like to offer some recommendations for the new board's consideration. As finances permit,
prioritize more substantial pay raises for educators with 20 plus years of experience, where KDISD
seems to lag. Continued and enhanced the teacher lump sum bonus program introduced this past year,
rewarding longevity and dedication.

[05:00–05:30]
Develop strategies to protect teacher planning time following recent board discussions. Reevaluate
the policies regarding fighting, physical contact, assault, and self-defense to ensure clarity and
fairness for students and staff. Revisit and strengthen the policy on naming of facilities to ensure
transparency, broad community involvement, and to safeguard against undue influence. Public service
is a noble call.

[05:30–06:00]
Service is a noble calling, but it also comes with great personal sacrifice and challenges.
Unfortunately, in today's climate, those who step forward to serve often face relentless personal
attacks, particularly on social media. The level of ugliness spewed on social media is out of
control. It's a darkness, a cancer. Constructive debate and differing opinions are vital to a
healthy democracy, but personal vilification serves no one and discourages good people from running
for school board. KDIFD belongs to the entire community.

[06:00–06:30]
not to a select few or an inner circle who seek to somehow maintain control or feel entitled. We
must reject the toxicity and recommit to civility and truthfulness and focus on what truly matters,
the well being of our students. Another critical issue is voter engagement. School board elections
held in May suffer from chronically low turnout, with participation rates of around 7%. This is a
disservice to our community. Moving these elections to November when voter

believe God has a plan. Thank you.
